last_digest.title: CISA flags Windows Task Host vulnerability as exploited in attacks
last_digest.rss_summary: CISA warned U.S. government agencies to secure their systems against a Windows Task Host privilege escalation vulnerability that could allow attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges. [...]

last_digest.title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
last_digest.rss_summary: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption.

last_digest.title: Microsoft pays $2.3M for cloud and AI flaws at Zero Day Quest
last_digest.rss_summary: Microsoft has awarded $2.3 million to security researchers after receiving nearly 700 submissions during this year's Zero Day Quest hacking contest. [...]

last_digest.title: Educational company McGraw Hill says Salesforce misconfiguration led to data leak
last_digest.rss_summary: The data breach emerged this weekend when the ShinyHunters cybercriminal organization claimed to have stolen 45 million Salesforce records and threatened to leak the information by April 14 if a ransom was not paid.

last_digest.title: ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks
last_digest.rss_summary:
Researchers warn that a flaw in Anthropic’s Model Context Protocol allows unsanitized commands to execute silently, enabling full system compromise across widely used AI environments.
The post ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

last_week_digest.title: Russian hackers hijack internet traffic using vulnerable routers
last_week_digest.tldr: Russian hackers, likely APT28, have compromised routers to hijack internet traffic, targeting devices including TP-Link and MikroTik models, with around 2 clusters of activity identified, allowing them to intercept browser sessions and collect authentication data, demonstrating the exploitation of vulnerabilities in widely used network devices.

last_week_digest.title: U.S. agencies alert: Iran-linked actors target critical infrastructure PLCs
last_week_digest.tldr: Iran-linked actors targeted internet-exposed PLCs in US critical infrastructure, causing disruptions and financial losses across sectors like government and energy, compromising at least 75 devices, including Unitronics PLCs, with potential targeting of other vendors like Siemens.

last_week_digest.title: Fast-moving Storm-1175 uses new exploits to breach networks and drop Medusa
last_week_digest.tldr: China-based Storm-1175 breaches networks, exploiting 16 vulnerabilities since 2023, and deploys Medusa ransomware, targeting healthcare, education, and finance sectors across the US, UK, and Australia, with attacks sometimes completed within 24 hours, causing significant business disruption and financial loss.

last_week_digest.title: UK exposes Russian cyber unit hacking home routers to hijack internet traffic
last_week_digest.tldr: British officials warned that Russian hackers are exploiting vulnerable home routers to hijack internet traffic, targeting small office and home office devices with weak security settings, affecting numerous users and potentially disrupting business operations, with the group gaining access via default or weak passwords and modifying DNS settings to intercept sensitive data.

last_week_digest.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt US Critical Infrastructure via PLC Attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Iran-linked hackers have disrupted US critical infrastructure by manipulating PLC and SCADA systems, triggering operational disruptions across multiple sectors, raising concerns over broader OT targeting, with federal agencies warning of potential further attacks, impacting business operations and continuity.

last_week_digest.title: Russia-linked APT28 uses PRISMEX to infiltrate Ukraine and allied infrastructure with advanced tactics
last_week_digest.tldr: Russia-linked APT28 used PRISMEX malware to infiltrate Ukraine and allies, targeting defense systems and aid infrastructure, exploiting flaws like CVE-2026-21509, with 11-day zero-day exploitation, and using stealthy techniques for espionage and command-and-control, affecting key regions like Kyiv and Kharkiv.

last_week_digest.title: Social engineering attacks on open source developers are escalating
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean hackers socially engineered an Axios maintainer, injecting malware into npm packages downloaded over 100 million times a week, with similar attacks targeting other open source developers, prompting an OpenSSF advisory warning of escalating social engineering attacks on developers, with potentially severe business consequences.

last_week_digest.title: Chaos malware expands from routers to Linux cloud servers
last_week_digest.tldr: Chaos malware has expanded from routers to Linux cloud servers, with a new variant compromising misconfigured servers, enabling DDoS attacks and proxy functionality, potentially generating revenue for operators, and altering the risk profile for organisations with cloud workloads, with around 64-bit ELF binary compiled for x86-64 Linux architecture.

last_week_digest.title: Two prominent Egyptian journalists targeted with elaborate spearphishing campaign
last_week_digest.tldr: Two Egyptian journalists were targeted in a sophisticated spearphishing campaign from October 2023 to January 2024, with attackers pretending to be legitimate services, but neither account was penetrated, highlighting the threat to global press freedom and the need for journalists to exercise digital caution, particularly in the Middle East and North Africa.

last_week_digest.title: 300,000 People Impacted by Eurail Data Breach
last_week_digest.tldr: Eurail's data breach affected 300,000 people, with hackers stealing names and passport numbers, resulting in significant operational consequences for the European travel company, potentially disrupting travel plans and damaging customer trust, with global implications for the industry.

last_week_digest.title: Eurail data breach impacted 308,777 people
last_week_digest.tldr: Hackers breached Eurail in Dec 2025, stole names and passport data, and exposed over 300,000 travelers’ personal information. Threat actors breached Eurail in December 2025 and stole names and passport numbers from its network. The company now notifies 308,777 people that attackers exposed their personal data, raising concerns about identity theft and misuse of sensitive […]

last_week_digest.title: New ‘LucidRook’ malware used in targeted attacks on NGOs, universities
last_week_digest.tldr: A new Lua-based malware, called LucidRook, is being used in spear-phishing campaigns targeting non-governmental organizations and universities in Taiwan. [...]

last_week_digest.title: Bitcoin Depot hack leads to $3.6M Bitcoin theft via stolen credentials
last_week_digest.tldr: Hackers breached Bitcoin Depot, stole credentials, and took about 50 BTC worth $3.6M from its wallets after a March 23 intrusion. Hackers breached the largest US Bitcoin ATM operator, Bitcoin Depot, on March 23, stole login credentials, and drained about 50.9 BTC worth $3.6M from company wallets. Bitcoin Depot told the SEC that a hacker […]

last_week_digest.title: Cryptocurrency ATM giant Bitcoin Depot reports $3.6 million stolen in cyberattack
last_week_digest.tldr: Bitcoin Depot filed a notice with the Securities Exchange Commission (SEC) explaining that a threat actor “gained access to certain systems and obtained control of credentials associated with the company’s digital asset settlement accounts.”

last_week_digest.title: Apple Intelligence AI Guardrails Bypassed in New Attack
last_week_digest.tldr:
RSAC researchers hacked Apple Intelligence using the Neural Exect method and Unicode manipulation.
The post Apple Intelligence AI Guardrails Bypassed in New Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

last_week_digest.title: Censys finds 5,219 devices exposed to attacks by Iranian APTs, majority in U.S.
last_week_digest.tldr: Censys researchers identified 5,219 internet-exposed Rockwell Automation PLCs globally, with 74.6% located in the United States. These vulnerable industrial systems, often running outdated firmware, face active exploitation by Iran-linked threat actors targeting critical infrastructure like water and energy sectors. This campaign highlights the severe operational risks posed by internet-facing operational technology, signalling an urgent need for organisations to disconnect exposed field devices and prioritise robust network segmentation.

last_week_digest.title: Poisoned “Office 365” search results lead to stolen paychecks
last_week_digest.tldr: The threat group Storm-2755 is targeting Canadian employees through sophisticated SEO poisoning and adversary-in-the-middle attacks to hijack Office 365 credentials. By bypassing traditional multi-factor authentication, attackers access corporate accounts to manipulate payroll records and divert salaries. This campaign highlights a critical shift toward exploiting trusted internal communication channels, signalling that organisations must urgently adopt phishing-resistant FIDO2 authentication and implement mandatory out-of-band verification for all sensitive financial data changes.

last_week_digest.title: In Other News: Cyberattack Stings Stryker, Windows Zero-Day, China Supercomputer Hack
last_week_digest.tldr: Medical technology giant Stryker recently confirmed a cyberattack, joining law firm Jones Day as a high-profile victim of escalating digital intrusions. These breaches disrupt critical operational continuity and threaten sensitive proprietary data, underscoring the severe financial and reputational risks facing global enterprises. Such incidents signal a broader industry shift where sophisticated threat actors increasingly target diverse sectors, necessitating more robust, proactive security postures to mitigate widespread systemic vulnerabilities.

last_week_digest.title: Nearly 4,000 US industrial devices exposed to Iranian cyberattacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Iranian state-backed hackers are actively targeting 3,891 internet-exposed Rockwell Automation programmable logic controllers across the United States, causing significant operational disruptions and data manipulation. This campaign highlights the severe risks of connecting critical industrial control systems directly to the public internet. The incident signals a growing trend of geopolitical actors weaponising vulnerable operational technology, forcing global organisations to urgently reassess their remote access security and network isolation strategies.

last_week_digest.title: CPUID site hijacked to serve malware instead of HWMonitor downloads
last_week_digest.tldr: CPUID recently suffered a six-hour backend breach that hijacked download links for popular tools like HWMonitor, exposing users to credential-stealing malware. By compromising a secondary API, attackers redirected visitors to malicious installers without altering the original signed software. This incident highlights a critical industry vulnerability where supply chain integrity is undermined at the delivery layer, signalling that businesses must secure peripheral web infrastructure as rigorously as core code.

last_week_digest.title: Rockstar Games receives “pay or leak” warning after cyberattack
last_week_digest.tldr: Rockstar Games confirmed a security breach after hackers accessed its Snowflake environment via a compromised third-party SaaS provider, Anodot. While the developer maintains that the incident involved limited, non-material data, the extortion attempt by the ShinyHunters group highlights the escalating risks of supply chain vulnerabilities. This event signals a broader industry trend where attackers bypass robust internal security by exploiting interconnected cloud services to gain unauthorized corporate access.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers claim breach of Rockstar Games via cloud analytics platform
last_week_digest.tldr: The cybercrime group ShinyHunters recently breached Rockstar Games by exploiting authentication tokens stolen from the cloud analytics platform Anodot. While Rockstar downplayed the incident as non-material, the attack highlights a broader supply-chain vulnerability affecting over a dozen organisations using Snowflake environments. This breach signals a critical shift in industry risk, where third-party service providers have become primary vectors for attackers targeting sensitive corporate data and intellectual property globally.

last_week_digest.title: OpenAI Impacted by North Korea-Linked Axios Supply Chain Hack
last_week_digest.tldr: OpenAI recently confirmed that a macOS code signing certificate was compromised following a supply chain attack linked to North Korean threat actors targeting the Axios platform. While the breach did not result in direct data loss for OpenAI users, the incident highlights critical vulnerabilities within software distribution pipelines. This event signals a growing industry trend where sophisticated state-sponsored groups increasingly exploit third-party dependencies to undermine global enterprise security.

last_week_digest.title: European Gym giant Basic-Fit data breach affects 1 million members
last_week_digest.tldr: European fitness giant Basic-Fit recently suffered a significant data breach, exposing the personal details of one million members across twelve countries. Attackers accessed sensitive information, including bank account numbers and contact data, highlighting critical vulnerabilities in centralised member databases. This incident underscores the growing risk to large-scale consumer service providers, signalling that even rapid internal detection cannot prevent substantial regulatory scrutiny and potential reputational damage within the European market.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers access Booking.com user data, company secures systems
last_week_digest.tldr: Booking.com recently confirmed a data breach where unauthorised parties accessed sensitive customer information, including names, email addresses, and reservation details. While payment data remained secure, the incident forced a mandatory reset of reservation PINs to mitigate further risk. This event highlights the persistent threat to global travel platforms, signalling an urgent need for enhanced third-party security oversight to prevent sophisticated phishing campaigns targeting customers via stolen booking data.

last_week_digest.title: Personal data of 1 million gym members compromised in Basic-Fit security incident
last_week_digest.tldr: Basic-Fit, Europe’s largest gym operator, recently suffered a significant data breach exposing the personal information of approximately one million members. Compromised details include names, addresses, bank account numbers, and contact information. This incident highlights the persistent vulnerability of large-scale consumer databases to unauthorised access. It serves as a stark reminder for the fitness industry that robust monitoring and rapid incident response are essential for protecting sensitive member data.

last_week_digest.title: Europe’s Largest Gym Chain Says Data Breach Impacts 1 Million Members
last_week_digest.tldr: Basic-Fit, Europe’s largest gym chain, recently suffered a significant data breach compromising the personal records of one million members. Attackers successfully exfiltrated sensitive information, including names, dates of birth, and banking details, creating severe operational risks and potential regulatory liabilities. This incident signals a growing trend of cybercriminals targeting consumer-facing service providers, highlighting the urgent necessity for robust data protection measures across the global fitness industry.

last_week_digest.title: McGraw-Hill confirms data breach following extortion threat
last_week_digest.tldr: McGraw-Hill confirmed a data breach resulting from a Salesforce misconfiguration, triggering extortion threats from the ShinyHunters group. While the company claims the exposed information is limited and non-sensitive, the attackers allege they possess 45 million records. This incident highlights critical systemic risks within third-party SaaS environments, demonstrating how configuration errors can expose global enterprises to significant reputational damage and extortion attempts despite robust internal security protocols.

last_week_digest.title: OpenAI expands its cyber defense program with GPT-5.4-Cyber for vetted researchers
last_week_digest.tldr: OpenAI is scaling its Trusted Access for Cyber program, granting thousands of verified defenders access to GPT-5.4-Cyber, a model fine-tuned for binary reverse engineering and vulnerability analysis. By lowering refusal boundaries for security professionals, the initiative aims to accelerate patch cycles and software resilience. This shift signals a broader industry trend toward providing defensive AI tools to combat sophisticated threats, balancing dual-use risks with critical infrastructure protection.

last_week_digest.title: No honor among thieves as 0APT threatens rival ransomware gang Krybit
last_week_digest.tldr: Ransomware gang 0APT has launched an extortion campaign against rival group Krybit, threatening to expose the identities of its affiliates. By leaking plaintext credentials and five cryptocurrency wallets, 0APT demonstrates a shift toward criminal-on-criminal warfare. This escalation signals a volatile cyber-threat landscape where internal instability among threat actors creates unpredictable risks, potentially exposing sensitive data and operational details as criminal enterprises increasingly target each other for leverage.

last_week_digest.title: CISA flags Windows Task Host vulnerability as exploited in attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: CISA has added a critical Windows Task Host privilege escalation vulnerability, CVE-2025-60710, to its actively exploited catalog, mandating that federal agencies patch their systems within two weeks. This flaw allows local attackers to gain full SYSTEM control over Windows 11 and Server 2025 devices. The development signals a broader industry trend where core system components are increasingly targeted, forcing organisations to accelerate patching cycles to mitigate significant operational risks.

last_week_digest.title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
last_week_digest.tldr: A six-year ransomware campaign has systematically targeted Turkish households and small-to-medium businesses, exploiting the relative anonymity of smaller-scale attacks to evade detection. By avoiding high-profile enterprise targets, the threat actors maintained persistent operations, causing significant operational disruption and financial losses for numerous victims. This enduring campaign signals a concerning industry shift, highlighting how attackers increasingly favour low-profile, long-term exploitation strategies to bypass traditional security monitoring and oversight.

last_week_digest.title: Microsoft pays $2.3M for cloud and AI flaws at Zero Day Quest
last_week_digest.tldr: Microsoft awarded $2.3 million to global researchers after identifying over 80 high-impact cloud and AI vulnerabilities during its Zero Day Quest event. This initiative highlights the critical necessity of proactive bug bounty programmes in securing complex infrastructure. By addressing potential cross-tenant access and credential exposure, the company aims to rectify historical security culture concerns, signalling a broader industry shift toward prioritising rigorous, collaborative testing for emerging technologies.

last_week_digest.title: Educational company McGraw Hill says Salesforce misconfiguration led to data leak
last_week_digest.tldr: McGraw Hill recently suffered a data breach linked to a widespread Salesforce misconfiguration, exposing non-sensitive information. While the company maintains its core systems remain secure, the incident follows claims by the ShinyHunters group regarding 45 million stolen records globally. This event highlights the critical operational risks posed by third-party platform configurations, signalling that even major enterprises must rigorously audit external vendor environments to prevent significant reputational damage.

last_week_digest.title: ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Researchers have identified a critical design flaw in Anthropic’s Model Context Protocol that permits unsanitized commands to execute silently, potentially enabling widespread supply chain compromises across global AI environments. This vulnerability threatens the integrity of automated workflows, risking unauthorised data access and system manipulation. The discovery signals an urgent need for robust security frameworks as organisations increasingly integrate AI agents, highlighting systemic risks inherent in rapidly expanding interconnected infrastructures.
